programmes.show_description: Current affairs with a comedic slant. Ed Byrne, Milton Jones, Russell Kane and Zoe Lyons join the mocking mainstays to begrudgingly reflect on Germany's World Cup win.
Bob Mortimer and Mel Giedroyc are amongst the stars revealing improbable titbits about themselves, some of which are untrue. Can their fellow celebs pick out the porkies?
Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guests Chizzy Akudolu, Simon Gregson, Michelle Visage and Henning Wehn.
Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster challenge more celebrities to square the theoretical circle - their guests this time are Ross Noble, Lou Sanders, Angela Barnes and Sunil Patel.
Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster are joined by Victoria Coren-Mitchell, Rosie Jones, Isy Suttie and Toussaint Douglass to test more theoretically absurd scenarios.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
A shipment of meth disguised as concrete is intercepted, and officers interview a Haitian immigrant who has crossed nine countries on foot to reach America.
Another One Bites The Dust: Drug Squad Sergeant Gerry and his team receive intel that three individuals are claiming responsibility for a primary school fire, a mission close to Gerry's heart.
Hide and Seek: Customs officers come across a suspicious boat, far out at sea at night, which they think is part of a smuggling operation and is refuelling the smugglers' speedboats.
Documentary series. Pacific National's Steel Train battles the Nullarbor to deliver its vital cargo across Australia, and a broken loco gets master mechanic Mick steaming.
Mallorca and Catalonia: Rick Stein discovers the delights of Mediterranean food, including seafood and roast lamb in Mallorca, and Catalonian food at Barcelona's Boqueria Market.
A man wanted for murder is apprehended at the border, and officers discover over 80 kilograms of cocaine hidden inside a tractor-trailer.
Officers investigate a collector travelling with parts of a space rocket, and 380 kilograms of crystal meth is discovered in a shipment of limes.
The history enthusiast honours Lancaster bomber crews of the Second World War as he tries out several onboard roles including pilot, gunner, and 'bomb aimer'.
More madcap motoring with the team, plus all the not-quite-latest news and views from the vehicular world. Jeremy tests an updated version of the classic Jensen Interceptor.
High jinks on the highway in the motoring show. Jeremy and James like to be beside the seaside in two new electric cars, and Richard meets the world's most extraordinary rally team.
Takeaways: The biker duo explore how Britain's love affair with takeaway food has evolved and cook some of their favourites in the Best of British kitchen.
Mallorca and Catalonia: Rick Stein discovers the delights of Mediterranean food, including seafood and roast lamb in Mallorca, and Catalonian food at Barcelona's Boqueria Market.
Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guests Shirley Ballas, Steven Bartlett, Josie Gibson and Chris McCausland for more amazing tales.
TV presenters Alex Jones and Karim Zeroual, comedian Jessica Knappett, and Judge Rob Rinder think they've got what it takes to take home one of the Pointless star's prizes this time.
Retired athletes Steve Cram and Jeanette Kwakye hope that they won't be for the high jump when they join comedians Lloyd Griffith and Josie Long on the lovable quiz show.
Unsavoury: Join Sandi Toksvig for a thoroughly unsavoury episode with Gyles Brandreth, Judi Love, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Alan Davies.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Victoria Coren Mitchell is host as comedian Chris McCausland and journalist Sophy Ridge join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Five series champions - Josh Widdicombe, Katherine Ryan, Noel Fielding, Rob Beckett and Bob Mortimer - clash as we find out their most embarrassing items.
English, Irish and Canadian comics take a totally unbiased look at Scottish independence as the likes of Ed Byrne and Josh Widdicombe hold the week's news up to ridicule.
Lee Mack, David Mitchell, Miles Jupp, Heston Blumenthal, Emilia Fox and Ed Byrne try to separate tall tale from candid confession in the wily panel show. Rob Brydon hosts.
Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guests Shirley Ballas, Steven Bartlett, Josie Gibson and Chris McCausland for more amazing tales.
Mel Giedroyc and Lou Sanders are joined by Rylan Clark, Lucy Beaumont and Fatiha El-Ghorri as Rylan has a sticky run-in with a pigeon and Lucy reveals her knicker-nicking past.
Mel and Lou Sanders are joined by Joel Dommett, Omid Djalili and Lindsey Santoro as Joel confesses some dastardly behaviour and Omid reveals how he upset Gwyneth Paltrow.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
A man wanted for murder is apprehended at the border, and officers discover over 80 kilograms of cocaine hidden inside a tractor-trailer.
This Could Go Bang At Any Time: The Drug Squad hunts for an illegal fuel stash hidden deep in the Rock of Gibraltar, and Customs officers find valuable intelligence in an abandoned boat.
Probably The Best Job In The World: Gibraltar's armed police officers arrest a man accused of making death threats to police, and Customs officers search a huge cargo boat from Colombia.
Documentary series. A group of trainee train drivers faces the reality of life on the lines, and Metro Train's Craigieburn Maintenance Depot battles to meet the network's demands.
Morocco and Turkey: As Rick's engaging guide to Mediterranean food reaches Morocco, he enjoys couscous, tucks into tagines and mint tea, and cooks the Moroccan dish of kefta mkaouara.
24 kilograms of cocaine are found concealed in the air tank of a truck, and a cache of weapons bound for Mexico is intercepted.
Officers intercept a shipment of hundreds of gun parts headed for Mexico, a nurse is caught smuggling pills in her boots, and a traveller carrying goat meat is inspected.
Engineering enthusiast Guy Martin recreates technical achievements of the 19th century and overhauls a steam locomotive currently in use on the Severn Valley Railway.
Motor mayhem. A trip to Italy for a supercar shootout featuring the Lamborghini Aventador, the McLaren MP4-12C and the Noble M600.
Four-wheeled fun. Richard heads to the US to compare F1 and NASCAR, Jeremy and James travel to Beijing to probe China's car industry, and future host Matt LeBlanc stops by.
Sweet Tooth: A sugary episode, as the hairy ones try their hand at making their favourite childhood sweets and make a truly sumptuous cheesecake.
Morocco and Turkey: As Rick's engaging guide to Mediterranean food reaches Morocco, he enjoys couscous, tucks into tagines and mint tea, and cooks the Moroccan dish of kefta mkaouara.
Rob Brydon hosts as team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guests Mist, Jayde Adams, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Ellie Simmonds.
TV presenters Alex Jones and Karim Zeroual, comedian Jessica Knappett, and Judge Rob Rinder think they've got what it takes to take home one of the Pointless star's prizes this time.
Retired athletes Steve Cram and Jeanette Kwakye hope that they won't be for the high jump when they join comedians Lloyd Griffith and Josie Long on the lovable quiz show.
Uncle Sam: Sandi Toksvig puts the UK-US special relationship under the microscope with Kemah Bob, Alex Edelman, David Mitchell, and Alan Davies.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Alex Horne is host as comedian Jack Dee and broadcaster Carol Vorderman join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Five hall of famers return for more consensual indignity at the hands of Greg Davies. The challenges this time include making a mask which both looks and tastes great.
